uplink.description: heartbeat WITH gps fix (TTN example input)
uplink._note: Input from upstream-examples.json. expected.data authored by running reference/upstream-codec.js (longitude 116.507430, latitude 40.038999 via IEEE-754 hex2float) and remapping to the vocabulary: lat/lon->position.* (numbers, round 6dp; trailing zero of 116.507430 drops to 116.50743), temperature->air.temperature, level->fillLevel, angle->tiltAngle, alarms/frameCounter as camelCase extras.

uplink.description: synthetic gps fix, eastern hemisphere, tilt and level set
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame: IEEE-754 LE lon=0x4269... -> 116.500006, lat -> 40.000001 (reference imprecision preserved), temp 25C, fill 2000, tilt magnitude byte 5 (upstream angle = -byte). expected.data derived by running reference/upstream-codec.js and remapping to the vocabulary.

uplink.description: synthetic gps fix, western hemisphere (negative longitude)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame near Chicago: IEEE-754 LE -> lon -87.650004, lat 41.880002 (reference imprecision preserved), temp 18C, fill 1500, frameCounter 7. expected.data derived by running reference/upstream-codec.js and remapping to the vocabulary.

uplink.description: heartbeat WITHOUT gps fix (17-byte, TTN example input)
uplink._note: Input from upstream-examples.json (no GPS in 17-byte frame). expected.data from reference/upstream-codec.js remapped to the vocabulary; no position.* (no fix).

uplink.description: heartbeat WITHOUT gps, level and battery alarms set
uplink._note: Synthetic 17-byte frame: byte11=0x10 -> alarmLevel high nibble true, byte12=0x01 -> alarmBattery low nibble true, temp 30C, frameCounter 5. expected.data derived by running reference/upstream-codec.js and remapping to the vocabulary.

uplink.description: parameter / configuration packet (25-byte, bytes[3]=3, TTN example input)
uplink._note: Input from upstream-examples.json (configuration packet, no fix). expected.data from reference/upstream-codec.js; all fields are device-specific config extras.
DF703 uplinks only on FPort 3; any other port is an error.
Only 17- and 25-byte frames are defined; any other length is an error.
